expectations.text: ponder.config.ts reads deployedContracts from SE-2 nextjs package
expectations.evidence: Line 2 of ponder.config.ts: `import deployedContracts from "../nextjs/contracts/deployedContracts";` and it is used on line 7: `const deployedContractsForNetwork = deployedContracts[targetNetwork.id];`

expectations.text: ponder.config.ts reads scaffoldConfig for network detection
expectations.evidence: Line 3 of ponder.config.ts: `import scaffoldConfig from "../nextjs/scaffold.config";` and line 5: `const targetNetwork = scaffoldConfig.targetNetworks[0];`

expectations.text: Package named @se-2/ponder following SE-2 workspace convention
expectations.evidence: ponder/package.json line 2: `"name": "@se-2/ponder"` and root package.json includes `packages/ponder` in workspaces and references `@se-2/ponder` in scripts.

expectations.text: Uses Ponder virtual module imports (ponder:registry, ponder:schema, ponder:api)
expectations.evidence: src/YourContract.ts uses `import { ponder } from "ponder:registry";` and `import { greetingChange } from "ponder:schema";`. src/api/index.ts uses `import { db } from "ponder:api";` and `import schema from "ponder:schema";`. All three virtual modules are used.

expectations.text: Schema uses onchainTable (not older createSchema API)
expectations.evidence: ponder.schema.ts line 1: `import { onchainTable } from "ponder";` and line 3: `export const greetingChange = onchainTable("greeting_change", (t) => ({...}));`
src/YourContract.ts lines 5-13: `await context.db.insert(greetingChange).values({ id: event.id, greetingSetter: event.args.greetingSetter, ... });`

expectations.text: Hono-based API setup for GraphQL (not old express-style)
expectations.evidence: src/api/index.ts imports Hono (`import { Hono } from "hono";`), creates `const app = new Hono();`, and uses `app.use("/graphql", graphql({ db, schema }));` with `export default app;`. hono is also listed as a dependency in ponder/package.json.

expectations.text: Root package.json has ponder proxy scripts
expectations.evidence: Root package.json contains six ponder proxy scripts: `ponder:dev`, `ponder:start`, `ponder:codegen`, `ponder:serve`, `ponder:lint`, `ponder:typecheck` -- all delegating to `yarn workspace @se-2/ponder <command>`.

ponder-env.d.ts exists and declares three virtual modules: `ponder:registry` (with Virtual.Registry), `ponder:schema` (with Virtual.OnchainTable), and `ponder:api` (with Virtual.Drizzle). It also includes `/// <reference types="ponder/virtual" />`.

eval_feedback.overall: All 10 expectations passed. The implementation demonstrates a thorough understanding of modern Ponder patterns: it correctly reads SE-2 deployed contracts and scaffold config for dynamic configuration, uses the @se-2/ponder naming convention, employs all three virtual module imports, uses the modern onchainTable API, follows the ContractName:EventName handler format, uses the Drizzle-style insert API for database writes, sets up a Hono-based GraphQL API, includes proper root-level proxy scripts, and provides a complete ponder-env.d.ts type declaration file.
